title: Molten Peppery Tunisian Red Pepper Tagine
description: A flavorful and spicy North African stew originating from Tunisia, characterized by the use of mortar and pestle to pound ingredients and creating a molten red pepper sauce. This adventurous dish is sure to add some excitement to your meal routine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay pot over medium heat.
Pound the onion, garlic, and ginger in a mortar and pestle until you get a smooth paste.
Add the cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to the mortar and pestle and mix well.
Add the red pepper flakes and harissa to the mortar and pestle and mix until you get a smooth paste.
Heat the olive oil in the clay pot and add the spice paste.
Saute for 1-2 minutes until the paste is fragrant.
Add the red bell peppers to the clay pot and saute for another 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 cup of water to the clay pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-12 minutes or until the peppers are tender.
Stir in the pitted green olives and cook for another 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a dollop of yogurt if desired.
If too thick, add a little more water to achieve the desired consistency.
If too thin, reduce the heat and simmer for a few more minutes.
